topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a stark intrusion into the otherwise quiet afternoon. My jaw tightened almost instantly. I didn't even need to look at the profile picture to know who it was; the familiar sneer was instantly recognizable, a phantom weight settling in my chest. My thumb hovered over the 'Decline' button, a muscle twitching in my hand. It felt like a deliberate provocation, a low-grade annoyance that demanded a response. I stared at the screen, the cool glass reflecting a sudden flush creeping up my neck. *Why now?* The thought was sharp, acidic. I deleted the request without a second glance, the action feeling like a necessary expulsion of something unpleasant.

[story 2]

When the alert chimed, I physically recoiled, pulling my phone away from my lap as if it were suddenly contaminated. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. The air in the room seemed to thicken, pressing down on my shoulders. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a telltale sign of rising tension. Looking at the name displayed—a name that used to conjure images of locker slams and whispered insults—made my stomach clench. I clenched my fists under the desk, knuckles turning white. I wanted to slam the device down, to shatter the screen, but I kept my hands still, forcing myself to breathe slowly, counting to ten, trying to anchor myself against the sudden, sharp surge of irritation flooding my system.

[story 3]

I picked up the phone slowly, the movement deliberate, almost reluctant. The sight of the request sent a jolt of unwelcome energy through me. A cold knot formed deep in my gut, tightening with every second I held the device in my grip. I scanned the profile, my eyes lingering on the familiar, mocking expression. A low, involuntary sound escaped my throat, a dry rasp. I felt a distinct pressure behind my eyes, a simmering frustration that made my jaw lock so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I tossed the phone onto the nearby surface with unnecessary force, the resulting clatter sounding loud in the silence, a small, sharp release of pent-up energy.
